title: proxy: OCI config digest is invalid due to remarshaling
body:
Originally from [composefs-rs](https://github.com/containers/composefs-rs/issues/91#issuecomment-2784496248)

tl;dr for above:  For OCI images the config json gets remarshalled in the proxy, which ends up emitting the document with the keys sorted alphabetically.  If the original config was sorted (typical for the `containers` stack it seems), then everything still works.  But the `alpine` image is an example of an OCI image where the config json keys are *not* sorted, so getting the config and validating the digest against the value in the manifest fails.

We attempted to [work around this](https://github.com/containers/composefs-rs/pull/98) in composefs-rs, but that exposed some other issues that I will track separately from this one.

comments.body: I don't have permissions to take this, but it can be assigned to me, I have WIP patch(es) for this.

Thanks for reaching out!

As discussed in that issue, the existing APIs are explicitly documented _convert to OCI_. Conversion from other formats will _typically_ change digests, so callers that assume the output will match the digest of the original are just not justified in making that assumption.

Sure, that does not rule out adding a new API.

---

This is a small enough project that assigning issues is not an important process — you’re welcome to just file a PR.
In the `alpine` case though, it's already in the correct format.  It just re-emits a semantically-identical config, except with the ordering potentially shuffled around for no reason.

In principle, the caller doesn’t _know that_. The registry could just as well have provided a non-OCI image (and it can start doing so in the future again), so the caller’s code path must be able to handle non-OCI inputs.

Even in the situations where the caller is absolutely positive (e.g. the input image uses a known digest reference), the API just doesn’t make that promise. We can add a new API that does have that property, and gives up on conversion. We can’t have an API that always achieves both simultaneously, so the caller _must_ be able to consume data which doesn’t have (at least) one of the two properties.

Minor correction to the original problem statement as I look at this more - it's the original `alpine` image in which the config keys are sorted; the converted config is unsorted.  That's reversed from what I originally said.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Apparently `composefs-fs` was changed to fetch the raw config instead using the “give me an OCI version” API.
